label: Condition
description: Evaluate a JavaScript expression and route to the matching downstream branch (true/false or custom labels).

settings.name: condition
settings.label: Expression
settings.description: JS boolean expression. `input` refers to the upstream output. {{steps.X.output}} template vars are resolved before evaluation.

ai.shortDescription: Branch on a JS boolean expression.
ai.whenToUse: When you need to route the workflow to different paths based on a condition — e.g. check if a value is above a threshold, if a field equals a string, or any JS boolean expression.
ai.examplePrompt: add a condition that checks if the HTTP status is 200
ai.promptHints: Use `input.field` to access upstream data. Connect the true/false output ports to different downstream nodes. Edge labels 'true' and 'false' are matched automatically.

outputAssertions.name: condition_branch_selected
outputAssertions.errorMessage: Condition node did not select a branch label — the routing decision is missing.

outputAssertions.name: condition_expression_evaluated
outputAssertions.errorMessage: Condition node's safe expression evaluator returned undefined — the expression failed to evaluate.
